# Composite exfil-suspicion score from upload ratio times peak megabytes (T1048)

**Dataset**: `gx_07_flows`

Builds a single composite suspicion score so a host with both a lopsided upload ratio AND a large single transfer ranks above one that has only one of those traits. It computes the out/in ratio, converts the peak flow to megabytes by `divide(to_float(peak_out), to_float(1048576))`, then multiplies the two and rounds - rewarding hosts that are both ratio-skewed and volume-heavy. Each arithmetic step is its own `alter` so intermediates (`ratio`, `peak_mb`) are reusable and inspectable; the literal divisor still needs `to_float` to keep the division floating-point.

```sql
dataset = gx_07_flows
| filter bytes_in > 0
| comp sum(bytes_out) as total_out, sum(bytes_in) as total_in, max(bytes_out) as peak_out by host
| alter ratio = divide(to_float(total_out), to_float(total_in))
| alter peak_mb = divide(to_float(peak_out), to_float(1048576))
| alter suspicion = round(multiply(ratio, peak_mb))
| filter suspicion > 100
| sort desc suspicion
| fields host, total_out, total_in, peak_out, ratio, peak_mb, suspicion
```
